MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-22-2025 Continued To May-11-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PMQ DEPOSITIONS AND RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS (ADDED FOR POSTING OF PART 2 OF 2 OF THE TENTATIVE RULING)
Off Calendar Mar-03-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Person Most Qualified Depositions And Responsive Documents
Off Calendar Mar-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE A MINOR, BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD LITEM, CAROL SANTONI-COSTANTINI, CAROL SANTONI-COSTANTINI'S Motion To Compel Person Most Qualified Depositions And Responsive Documents

Plaintiff John Doe's motion to compel person most qualified depositions and responsive documents is granted. Doe, a high-school student alleges that defendant San Francisco Unified School District failed to protect him from assaults by other students. Doe now seeks to compel the deposition of, and production of documents by, SFUSD's PMQ concerning various topics. These topics, and the documents that are sought related to these topics, touch on information about other students. The parties agree that SFUSD's records of students are protected by federal and state education-privacy laws and cannot be produced without court order.

For good cause shown in the moving papers, the court grants Doe's motion and orders SFUSD to comply with the subpoena, including its documents requests, except that SFUSD may redact personally identifiable information of third-party students who are not the "assailant," as that term is defined in the deposition notice. The court orders that any information disclosed pursuant to the subpoena that is subject to federal and state education-privacy laws shall be restricted as follows: It shall be designated confidential; it may be used by Plaintiff's counsel only in connection with this litigation; it may be disclosed only to the parties, their attorneys, and any experts retained in connection with the litigation; before disclosure to any of the aforementioned persons, the receiving person must agree to be bound by the terms of this protective order; it must be destroyed within 90 days after this case is finally concluded; and if the court must consider any confidential information in order to rule on a motion or other request for relief, that information must be lodged under seal and accompanied by a motion to seal pursuant to CRC 2.550 and 2.551.

SFUSD's time to comply and make production of documents shall be 20 calendar days after entry of this order, or the next business day thereafter if the date falls on a weekend or holiday. Doe asks the court not to give time for SFUSD to notify parents or guardians of students whose private information is subject to disclosure. But disclosure to parents or guardians is required under applicable statutes; this order does not override those obligations.

SFUSD asks the court to receive parent/guardian objections in the first instance. The court denies that request. Parents/guardians may move to quash the subpoena and SFUSD is given leave to make a further motion to quash in accord with any parent/guardian objections it receives.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's Demurrer to Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Before the court is Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's demurrer to Plaintiff John Doe's First Amended Complaint. The demurrer seeks dismissal of Plaintiff's dangerous condition of public property claim. In lieu of substantive opposition, Plaintiff has requested an order authorizing his filing of a Second Amended Complaint, a copy of which is attached to the Declaration of Seth Rosenberg. Defendant does not oppose Plaintiff's request but asks for leave to withdraw its demurrer if the request is granted. Plaintiff's request for leave to file the Second Amended Complaint is granted. Plaintiff shall submit a proposed order so stating forthwith. Defendant's request for leave to withdraw the demurrer is granted and the demurrer is ordered off calendar.

Plaintiff must prepare and submit submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
